description:
From Rules:

The Space Travel Hazards game was developed to help reinforce knowledge of space travel and some of the potential hazards associated with space travel.

The object is to get from Earth to Mars and back along one of two pathways to accomplish a successful mission.  Along the way, teams acquire Radiation Points (RPs) that are detrimental to the mission.  Teams will also acquire Mission Points (MPs) that measure the success of the mission.  When the game is finished, plot the total Radiation Points and Mission Points on the Tally Card to see if the team had a successful mission and determine the game winner.

Components:
- copy of game procedures and rules
- copy of game board sheet
- Astronaut game pieces, 1 per team
- Chace Cards
- Tally Card
- One 6-sided die
- Pencil
- Score Chart (graph)
designers: United States National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

description:
Le Jeu du Berger (which means The Game of the Shepherd) comes with two different variants the first one is possible from the age of three and is called 'Cours, petit mouton, cours!' (Run, little sheep, run!) and is 100% luck. Here the players have to throw a die and put the sheep on the free space with the same numbers as the die roll. The child who can place all his sheep the first wins.

The second version is called 'Petit mouton, rentre à la maison! (Little sheep come home!) and this one is possible for players aged four. Here all the sheep are placed on spots with a number ranging from one to six one. Now the children roll the die and they may remove a sheep every round but the sheep may only be removed if the number that the sheep is standing on is the same as the die roll. This way the game becomes a memory game. The child who obtains the most cheep wins.

Roule Cocotte is a game about chickens that lay eggs. First the players set up the board by putting eggs in the spaces on the board. They can put up to six eggs in one space but there are not that much eggs so some spaces will only have one or two eggs and some up to six. Then they put the wooden chicken on top of all the eggs. Now the players run a die each turn. After each die roll they can lift a chicken and take the eggs below the chicken; but they may only take it if the roll equals the amount of eggs below that chicken.
Cycling game produced in the UK in the 1970's. What makes this game stand out is that the riders are magentic and no dice are used for movement; instead a spinning wheel is used.

Italian cycling game published in the 1960's. Up to five players each have a metal rider; one player acts as the bookie. Players then place wagers on the five riders who race to the finish line. The player with the most money after the agreed upon number of races is the winner.

French two player game about the Tour de France. It consists of 48 tiles. 28 of them form the road, and must be played in order. The other 20 cards are landscape cards and are placed with one of their sides touching an already placed card.

The winner is the first to play the Paris card as long as he does not have any other card in his hand. Nevertheless, players take cards from the other player's hand once per turn, so the Paris card will change hands every once in a while.

Quick Play Edition.  Half the time.  All the fun.

Can you get to the treasure first?  Roll the dice and spell a word to advance through the shark-infested waters.  Roll a 4-letter word to earn the eyepatch.  The first player to reach the treasure while wearing the eyepatch wins!  Roll and spell your way to victory, matey!  Arrgh!

Ialian cycling game from the 1950's. Comes with a board that depicts Italy with roads between cities. The players move their riders (by roling two wooden dice) on the road. The game starts in Milan; the first one to reach Rome is declared the winner.
Early 20th century card game with a biblical theme.

Based on a tv series with the same name. The board represents different locations (the docks, police station, the apartment of Cordelia, a cemetery, ...) in Los Angeles. One player plays with the character Wolfram & Hart (the advocates of the devil). The other players play with Angel and his acolytes (Cordelia, Gunn & Wesley).

The principle of the game is to win points by fulfilling mission cards, these points are needed for the destiny track. The ultimate goal of the game is to advance the soul of Angel on the destiny track to level six (six points).
family: TV Shows: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

description:
"Taste Taiwan" is a series of thick postcard game relevant to culture and geography in Taiwan. The first game is "Taste Taiwan: Warrior". "Princess" is the second game of this series.

By adding "Warrior", it will become a four-players game. You need to match couples of different tribes during the game or you will get minus points. Each couple will give an additional score if you finish your mission on the map board.

Can be combined with:

    Taste Taiwan: Warrior

From the box: Terry Terrier is in charge of looking after the dangerous lion, Leo. But "oh dear" - Leo has done a bunk!
Where has he gone? Terry immediately sets off to look for him. He retraces Leo’s escape route, from animal to animal asking them if they have seen the lion. But where does Leo’s track end? Who can help Terry find Leo’s hiding place?

Gameplay: The players open up the cards looking from card to card in the correct order to find the player where "Leo" has his hide-out. The fastest wins.

The "Sechs Sonderaufträge" (Six special commissions) are part of the Abacusspiele spring catalogue 2010 and part 10 in the expansion-series "catalogue edition".

With these commissions you have the option to choose where to deliver and which craftman you take for that.

Expands:

    Valdora

This is a game for 2 to 6 players on the now legendary 'Great Train Robbery'. On the 8th August, 1963 the travelling post office train was hijacked at Sears Crossing, Buckinghamshire, England. The train driver and co-driver were captured and the train driven a quarter of a mile to a bridge over the road. The mail vans were ransacked by the gang and the 110 mail bags were thrown down to a waiting lorry. The gang then went to Leatherslade Farm, their base for the share out. This was only 20 miles from the hold up.

Based on the actual events of the Great Train Robbery in 1963, this game is a realistic re-enactment of the chase making use of skill, judgement, initative and like life itself, a measure of luck.

A turn is comprised of rolling the dice three times in an attempt to create the patterns listed in the nine rows you set up at the beginning of the game. If you complete a row, you get to draw a coin from the bag to see which treasure you’ve won! You only need match the number on the tiles, but if any of your dice match both the suit and the number, you’ll win bonus draws from the bag. If your roll does not match anything on the board, you have the option of discarding a treasure to take another turn. If you choose not to do this, the game ends and the score is tabulated.

boardgame: Neu-erfundene Historisch-Chronologische Spiel-Tafel zur Erlernung der Teutschen Reichs-Historia nach der Regierungs-Folge der Römisch-Teutschen Kayser
description:
A game with 49 circles where one can see German Royals. In the middle of the gameboard one can see Charles VI (October 1685 – 20 October 1740), who was the penultimate Habsburg sovereign of the Habsburg Empire.

Second edition published 1726.

Third edition 1727.

There are strange things going on in your basement laboratories. They keep calling up for more barrels of quicksilver, or bits of your hair. Well it's all in the name of progress. They're looking for a way to turn lead into gold, or at least into something better than lead. That lead had just been too good of a bargain to pass up; you didn't think, where will I put all this lead, what am I going to do with this lead anyway. Well that will all be sorted out. They're also looking for a universal solvent. If they manage that one, you will take whatever they use to hold it in and build a castle out of it. A castle that can't be dissolved! Now that's progress.

Dominion: Alchemy is an expansion, and can't be played by itself; to play with it, you need Dominion a standalone expansion to Dominion (Dominion: Intrigue), or Dominion Base Cards . Those provide the Basic cards you need to play (Treasure, Victory, and Curse cards), as well as the full rules for setup and gameplay. Dominion: Alchemy can also be combined with any other Dominion expansions you have.

Part of the Dominion series.

description:
The battle by Union forces to retake the planet Bey was difficult at best, a slaughter at the worst, as the resistance encountered against the robotic Morpheans proved to be more difficult than expected.  After months of fighting, the Unions 15th  Combined Army commanded by Marshall Jean Baptiste, managed to break the Morphean siege lines among the hills and mountains of the sprawling Blade River valley.

The Blade River valley ran for hundreds of miles, and at dozens of locations throughout it, fierce battles took place for possession of key locations.  Bridges, power-plants, military training bases, and transportation docks were its lifeblood and the focal point of the fighting.  The 15th CA was reinforced by Union shock troops and mechs, enabling them to push the Morpheans back and eventually out of the valley area.  Mustering it's reserve units, the Morpheans responded in force, not willing to concede defeat to the inferior humans.  Wave upon wave of Morphean aero-fighters and bombers swept up the river valley in a final attempt to re-secure their dominance.

The Game

Bomber Run on Blade River is a 1-8 player game where the player or players take on the role of the skeletal Morphean robots with their aero-fighters and bombers, or the defending Union force with their missile equipped infantry squads, armored walkers, anti-aero guns and counter-fighters.

Attack on Blade River Valley is a free PnP consisting of a 9 page rulebook, 4 map pages (when all are combined), and a sheet of unit counters.  In addition, the player will need about 6-8 d6.

The game was designed to be played at work station or table, depending on how many of the map pieces the player/s wish to use.  When all the map pieces are placed together they cover an area the size of 4 sheets of 8.5x11 paper.  The simplest form of the game uses just one sheet (To be added shortly).

boardgame: "Malbrough s'en va-t-en guerre": Wargame Rules for The War of the Spanish Succession
description:
Malbrough s'en va-t-en guerre, this set of table-top and campaign rules are designed to be used to represent the Wars of the Spanish Succession.

The systems are adaptable to any scale between 2mm & 28mm.

There are two elements to the game: A Campaign game using a set of playing cards and the resulting figure battle. 
The former will give the latter its context and players must be careful to judge that the fight is worth the candle as the epitome of Generalship was the avoidance of risky battles.

If players wish, a simple system can be generated to create one-off battles, so that each battle is different or to get players used to the novel battle system.

This Gamebook contains:


     A brief summary of the period and details of the forces of the participants.



     A set of battlefield rules that can be used to recreate either the early or late period battles.



     A4 Coloured Sheet of Counter: Stick to card or foamboard and cut out



     Weekly Weather Sheet of Counters: Stick to card or foamboard and cut out



     2 x A4 Army Sheets: One for each side. Used for placing your contingents and generals on.



     2 x A4 Contingent & Army Morale Sheets



     A4 Year, Battlefield Clock & Victory Point Chart: 


—description from the publisher

description:
"Its just like checkers but..." 
The Checkers III playing board is a hexagon divided into 96 triangular playing spaces, 48 dark and 48 light. The last player left on the playing board, following a series of capturing and trapping moves similar to checkers, but based on triangular movement, is the winner.

A game of strategy, money and people.

The object of the game is to secretly acquire the most money in your personal account so that when the game is ended you will be declared the winner. Personal money accounts are kept secret throughout the game. Each character assumes the parodic character of 80s infamy (such as Daniel Dictator, Colonel South and Ayatollah Iraney) to accomplish their indivual ends.

boardgame: The Adventures of Dick and Virginia
description:
Give Your Best Advice

Dick and Virginia are a couple that suffer from a plague of sex and relationship problems. As their closest and most trusted friend, they have come to you for advice. Will your great advice help this couple keep it together through thick and thin or will an opponent swoop in with their two cents and become the couple's closest confidant? It takes critical thinking and an open mind to help this troubled couple. Find out how well your advice is received and how other players handle sex and relationship quandaries.

Directions:
Shuffle the deck and place face down. The first player draws the top card and gives advice to the fictitious couple. If another player wants to challenge the advice given, they may. The other players vote on the best advice and the winner gets the card. Each card is worth one point.

When a player collects five cards a round is complete. Depending on the number of players the amount of rounds changes. Once all rounds are complete the player with the most cards wins.

A wild Party game that is tame enough to play at almost any adults only party, but goes the distance for even the most non-vanilla players. It is intellectually and physically challenging for both couples and singles, and is overflowing with thought provoking and sexually stimulating challenges.

Designed for all Genders and Preferences
Includes non-gender specific game play that allows for comfortable play among homosexual, bisexual, and heterosexual players. The cards have been carefully written so that all contact events can be played with your monogamous partner whether or not he or she is on your team. For non-monogamous players, there has never been a better ice breaker.

Great for Team Competition
Split the party into teams and let the battle begin. Play girls vs. the boys and find out which gender is sexually smarter and braver. Or break down the teams and go couple vs. couple to discover which couple has the best sexual relationship.

Great for Couples
Every Thought card is carefully designed to engage the party in constructive conversations regarding the most common sexual and relationship problems. Every Action card was designed to reveal the most common sexual fantasies of both men and women. Playing this game with your spouse might just stoke the flames and teach you something about your partner that you never knew before playing.

Great for Singles
From college students to retirees, anyone who admits to being a sexual being can learn something from playing this game. In one hour of playing this game, you'll learn more about your sexual compatibility with other players than you could in a solid week of blind dates.

Directions:
Divide the players into teams. Separate the cards into the four decks of "Thoughts", "Actions", "Sex Survey", and "Daredevil". The first player on the first team chooses one card from the four decks and completes the challenge. If the other team thinks they have a better answer they may challenge. Each completed card counts as one point. Daredevil card points are marked individually.

The game ends when one deck is fully depleted and each team tallies their score to determine the winner.

description: French board game about cycling. The game is a dice game, but an ambitious one, which simulates all the aspects of road cycling. As with most racing games; the player who reaches the finish line first wins.

Get Ready to try new things.
Whether you are a woman interested in trying some girl-on-girl activities or a guy who'd like to be buried in up to his eyeballs in warm naked breasts, this game has something for you.

Play as couples or go solo
Share a hand with your partner and play the game as a couple or go it alone for maximum multiple partner possibilities.

Strategy wins the game
This game isn't just about the sex, it is also about screwing over your opponents and playing your hand smarter than the rest. Special cards can be used to double score, steal points and trade hands. Knowing how, when and with whom to fulfill sexual challenges is necessary to win the game. This game is like bridge or poker if bridge and poker were all about sex.

Directions:
The game may be played as two-person-teams or singles. Each player gets five cards. The first player chooses a card from their hand. Once the challenge is complete they score.

Any player may choose to give up their turn and draw five new cards.

The game is over when there are no more cards to draw or when one player is out of cards.

The winner is the one with the most points

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Monopoly - in pink.

A compendium of four family games:


    Primus Ludus. The aim of the game is to get one pawn of each colour (red, blue, yellow) to the center of the board. All players can move all pawns. The choice of pawn and direction is limited by two coloured dice. After rolling them, you have to move a pawn of one's colour along a line of the other's colour.
    Sekundus Ludus. You roll dice to move along a path. Depending on the field you reach, you either gain or lose sticks. If you reach the finish with insufficient sticks, you have to do another round.
    Tertius Ludus. An easy roll-and-move game, in which you have to get around the board with 3 of your 4 pawns. Similar to Pachisi, you may return other pawns to the start, too. The mechanic that makes the game special is the unlimited number of rolls you can make each turn. If you choose to roll another die, you can add the new score to your movement rate, but if you have bad luck, you lose everything and can't move this turn.
     Quartus Ludus. A card game based on the Rock-Paper-Scissors mechanic. A joker beats rock, paper, and scissors. Every time you lose to another card, your pawn is advanced. The one who reaches the end fist, loses the game.
In addition to including components for a fifth and sixth player, this expansion includes 216 new puzzles (= 18 new cards * 2 faces * 6 possibilities per face )

boardgame: Race for the Galaxy: The Brink of War
description:
The Brink of War is the third expansion to Race for the Galaxy and requires both earlier expansions (The Gathering Storm and Rebel vs Imperium).

This expansion adds 4 new start worlds, 5 goals, 44 game cards, and rules for galactic prestige. New solitaire game counters and drafting rules for six players are also provided.

Each round the player(s) with the most prestige earn an additional victory point and possibly a card. Each prestige is worth one victory point at the end of the game, and many of the new cards allow prestige to be spent (for victory points, cards, or other effects). Additionally, a new "once-per-game" action is introduced that allows the user to search the deck for a card meeting a specific stated requirement, or to enhance the bonus the user earns in a round (at the cost of one prestige).

description:
The instructional objective of the card game is for students to practice using Spanish vocabulary in comprehensible sentences.

The deck needs to be shuffled, then placed face down and separated. The first player turns over two cards. If one card is a vocabulary word that correctly fits into a sentence of the other card (for example sun and When it's hot, the _____ shines in the sky), the player gets to keep both cards and continue with his/her turn until he/she doesn't find a matching pair. If the cards do not match, the player must turn the cards face down again and the next player takes a turn. The rest of the players continue the game in the same manner.
The game is played until all the cards have been matched and there are no remaining cards. The winner is the person with the most cards at the end of the game.

Components:
32 cards
Sixteen of the cards will contain a single vocabulary word. The other 16 cards will contain a sentence with a blank.

The players will be able to reasonably classify 8 cultural characteristics that could possibly develop within a given biome.

The objective of the game is to get one of each of the eight categories. Each player draws and discards until one of the teammates has eight different categories. This player lays down his or her cards face up.
The other teammates and the "winner" must use only the cards in the players' hands to now create in the game board the most logical, consistent culture possible based upon the cards available and then analyze their culture.

Components:
48 cards

The square black plastic board has 8 x 8 spaces which are coloured indents that accommodate a player's black or white marbles.  The indents are coloured blue, green, red or yellow.  On their turn a dice is rolled and the player places their marble in an indent of the colour shown on the dice.  The aim is for a player to create a 3 x 2 rectangle of their own coloured balls.  The dice also includes two 'joggle' sides that allow players to move or swap pieces, but not to complete a winning move.

The players will practice simple addition and subtraction to collect as many cards as possible.

There are 2 face-up cards in the middle of the table.
The player turns over his top card and mentally adds or subtracts the amount with that on the start card. If the result does not match, the next player plays his top card, combining the amount with the previous running tally. The plays continue until a player matches the target or cards run out. In the first case, the player making the match is the winner. In the second case, no one is the winner.

40 cards

Players will have fun "hunting" while learning the food chains of several different regions.

The game has different variants to play. It can be played like War, or it can be played like Concentration

The players shall be able to correctly build new words by adding a prefix and/or suffix to existing base words.

Each player in turn takes the top card from the draw pile OR from the discard pile, not both. If the player can form one or more words, the words are laid on the table in front of that player. Words must have a base word+prefix or suffix or both. (dis+respect) (respect+ful) (dis+respect+ful)
Scores are calculated at the end of the game. The total point values of the words in front of each player are added, along with any bonuses. Point values of the cards remaining in a player's hand are subtracted from the total. Each word which consists of a root word plus two more word parts i.e.: a prefix AND a suffix earns a 5-point bonus. The person with the highest point total wins

The players shall be able to make associations among properties that determine unique classes of quadrilaterals and identify those shapes.

The object is to acquire the most points by playing combinations of properties that determine unique categories of quadrilaterals.
The game is over when all cards have been played. Individuals that go out ahead of others simply lose their turn.

The learner will be able to group picture cards the include the letter "r" according to whether the "r" is in the initial, medial, or final position of the word and will be able to pronounce the names of the pictures correctly.

The learners are students who are receiving speech therapy services because of difficulty producing the "r" sound correctly. They are kindergarten through third grade students.

Players group the cards into sets of three or more. As each card is placed on the table, the name of the picture must be stated. If it is not stated correctly, the learner must pick up the cards and attempt again next turn.

The winner is the first player to lay down all his/her cards in groups of three or more.

Components:

After playing the Recycle card game three or more times, players will be able to name all the recyclable items used in the Recycle card game.

Players ask each other for cards that they need to make 3, or 4 of a kind. If the other players do not have the card in question, they must draw card from the remaining undealt cards in the middle of the table. Once three or four of the same value cards with same recyclable are obtained, the cards can be claimed by laying them down face up on the table. The first person to get rid of all their cards wins that hand and scores points for 3 and 4 of a kinds.
The first person to reach 500 or more points is the winner. If more than one person reaches or goes over 500 points, the person with the most points wins.

The game, similar to "Gin Rummy", is played in the following manner:

The object of the game is to form three sets from a dealt hand of nine cards. 
The players chooses either the discarded card or a card from the face down pile. The player determines which cards to keep and discards the extra card. The first player to form three valid sets from ten cards wins the game.
The Use of "Mona Lisa" Cards. The deck contains three Mona Lisa cards which are wild cards. A player may substitue them for any card.

Components:
57 cards

The learners will be able to properly sequence the procedural steps for a professional rescuer.
The learners will be able to answer specific questions about each procedural step.
The learners will be able to differentiate between infant, child and adult emergency care.

Each player is dealt 7 cards. The remaining cards are placed face down in front of the dealer to form a draw pile. The top card of the draw pile is turned over and placed in the center of the circle. This card marks the first link in the beginning of the RESCUE ME! chain.
Then the player places a card, called a link, next to the first card. This card must show either the next or previous step the rescuer takes to help his or her victim. Also player must answer the question on the card correctly.
Once a player has no cards left, the game is over.

The players will be able to recognize economic resources and the category type of each one.

The object of the game is to get two groups of three and four cards of 2 different resource categories. The player determines which cards to keep and discards the extra card. The first player to create two groups of three and four cards places the cards down and must name the two different categories (e.g., capital, labor) shown. The other players refer to a reference card that indicates all the items in their respective categories to verify whether the player is correct. If the player cannot name the two categories, the player on his/her left can name them correctly to win the round. The player with the winning hand gains one point. The game is over when a player reaches a predetermined score. If a time limit is set instead, the winner is the player with the highest score at the end of the time.

The players will be able to identify musical key signatures and their respective primary chords and will be able to define musical dynamics terms.

The game follows the format of the card game "UNO".
Player has to match the card in the Discard pile by placing down a card with the same key signature or a primary chord card of that key signature. Or, the player can throw down a Wild Card to change the key signature. First, however, the player must correctly define the musical term on the card. If the player cannot identify the term, they must draw a card from the Draw pile
When a player has no cards left, they win that game.

Description:
Inevitable is a satirical board game set in a slapstick dystopian future.  Global civilization as we know it was completely obliterated.  Fortunately, HappyCOM-9 was created to rebuild society and successfully created The City.  Unfortunately, HappyCOM-9 is completely insane.  The City is a surreal and dangerous place where anvils intermittently drop from the sky and death is only an inconvenience (if you have the money to buy a clone).

There are many powerful groups in The City, like the Church, Ninjas, and the Post-Apocalyptic Beasts.  HappyCOM-9 has announced that there will be an election and that any group that wants to nominate a candidate to run against it may do so.  You are one of those candidates.

Your goal is to increase your power, influence, and intelligence so you are strong enough, popular enough, and smart enough to beat HappyCOM-9 in an election.  During your campaign for President, you'll buy fantastic items (rocket-powered roller skates, flamethrowers, etc.) from the Catalog and compete in Game Shows (minigames within the game, like You Bet Your Clone!).

description:
From the Box:

Connect the Flags. Claim the Squares. A classic game with a new twist. When you have 4 flags around a square, you claim it with a colored flag. Then twist the flags to make a new square and keep on going. The player with the most territory wins.

From the Box:

In this silly game you try to get rid of your Neckties first by colour-matching the character shirts. Too bad you don't know what color ties you have.

This is a turn based, computer-aided, hybrid boardgame. Played by email and 100% computer-moderated, it needs however a Game Master to enter the players moves, manage the correspondence, answer the questions, and so on. Although you can play it with paper and pencil only, the moves are usually entered by the players thanks to a free client program, in order to avoid an extra-work for the GM, and speed up the game. Difficult to categorize, Adventurer Kings is set in a fantasy world filled with heroes, intrigue, war and adventure, in which you can achieve victory in lots of different ways: military conquest, the slaying of monsters, the search for hidden treasures and magical items, exploration, and more.

From the Box:

The Ultimate Interactive Theme Party Game! Introducing Thoroughbred and Standardbred Harness Horse Racing in the comfort of your own home. Virtual Derby includes a VHS videotape that contains over 20 actual horse races, an instruction book, a game board, horse markers, wagering pads, Derby Dollars, and official racing sheets. For experienced players, interactive wagering account and cable/satellite service (at additional cost) information and interactive code charts are included.

At the Beginner level, the game is played with the VHS tape of actual horse races. Review the official racing sheet and pre-race odds, select horses to wager on, view the race, and payout the winners in Derby Dollars.

At the Experienced level, the game is played with the game board, wagering pads, live televised horse races and the setting up of a real time wagering account through Virtual Derby's website.

From the Box: A game of Romance and the Senses. Romantic Sensations is a card game that combines and reinforces our senses of touch, taste, scent, sound, and sight to create a wonderful romantic experience.

To start, players read the list of prizes--romantic, sensuous activities to be enjoyed together. The cards are shuffled and dealt. Players then take turns picking a card from the deck, reading it aloud, and responding. Some cards ask for verbal responses, positive and supportive. Others suggest gentle touching, some playful, some subtle and ambiguous. When like cards are matched, they are placed down. The first player to match the required number of cards wins.

Note: the game box includes two small bottles of lotion ("sensuous massage lotion" and "bath gel") in addition to the question card deck.

Theme

Not to mention that opposing warlord across the chasm forming his or her own teams to combat yours. Only the shrewdest of warlords will emerge from this feud victorious."

Mechanics
The two players create small armies of fighters by playing card groups (melds). Then attacking player commits is fighters to the battle and so does the defender. Players alternate in attacking and defending until one of the two has captured 13 or more enemies.

Themed Rummy game created for the 2009 Contest.

Artscow Deck

In the basic game, the dealer shuffles the cards and then play begins with each player being dealt seven cards, one at a time, all face down. The remaining deck is placed aside.  Players pick up the cards and hold them in their hands or place them face-up on the table.  Using the alphabet letters on the cards, players create and writes down the word with the highest number of points.  Alphabet letters can be used more than once.  A player declares his/her words to the others, who then confirm each word and tally the points for that word.
 In the advanced game, players may choose to discard cards from their hands and re-fill with new cards from the deck.

boardgame: Made in the USA the Game
description:
Made in the USA is different than any other game you have ever played; it combines elements of card and board games and dominoes. Players create four lines of play using the four different patriotic suits. Players have the freedom to play as many cards as connect, or unit, to each other. For example, you may play a Seven, Eight and Nine of Stars, then play an Eight of Eagles. Play cards that connect to other players' cards. But when you change lines/suits then you will need to draw a "Changes in the Wind" card - so even when you think you have won, the round might not be finished.

User review: Each player is dealt a hand of cards. The number of cards dealt to each player varies with the number of participants. In turn, a player can add to the cards in the array in the middle of the table. Cards of the same rank are placed in the same row. The cards must be ranked in ascending order from top to bottom. Cards of the same suit must be placed in the same row. Event cards may be played at the beginning or end of one’s turn. The "Changes in the Wind" cards are a separate draw pile. These cards are drawn whenever a player begins a new row.
The hand is over when one player’s hand is depleted. The other players are penalized for cards remaining in their hands. The game is over when someone has been penalized 500 points. The player with the lowest score is the winner.

A game for toddlers (1 1/2 to 3 yrs).  The goal is to get the dog home to his water bowl.  Basically, it's a memory game with only 3 different pictures.

The game box becomes the dog's yard and a 3-d dog house is built.  It's only real function is to entertain the toddler.

Some of London's main streets are familiar to a host of children and adults through the Monopoly board game, with almost everyone recognising the names of Mayfair, Park Lane and Bond Street.
This Christmas, the towns and cities of Wales will become equally as familiar, with a new Welsh-language version of the game. Cardiff Bay will replace Mayfair and the Menai Straits will take over from Park Lane.
In welcoming the new Welsh-language version, Rhodri Glyn Thomas, Minister for Heritage, said: 'We are extremely pleased that the Books Council have been able to support the Welsh-language Monopoly and its success will not only bring pleasure to many this Christmas, but will also enable families across Wales to play this popular board game through the medium of Welsh.'
Winning Moves, who have produced a number of versions of the popular board game based on different regions of Britain, are pleased to add a distinctive Welsh-language version to the collection, with the players winning or losing their fortune as they travel the length and breadth of Wales.
The company invited the Books Council to assist them in selecting appropriate names for the game board, and the Council sought the assistance of the leading language expert D. Geraint Lewis, who has published numerous dictionaries and grammar books.
'We were delighted to be able to work with Winning Moves to produce the Welsh-language version of this popular board game,' said Gwerfyl Pierce Jones, Director of the Welsh Books Council.
'The Welsh-language version of Scrabble proved to be a best-seller and we are sure that Monopoly yn Gymraeg (Monopoly in Welsh) will have similar success amongst Welsh speakers and Welsh learners of all ages. The whole process was a most pleasing challenge and D. Geraint Lewis's contribution as a consultant was a key factor in ensuring that the game reflected all the different regions of Wales.'
A set of both English- and Welsh-language rules are included with each game, to assist Welsh learners.
D. Geraint Lewis added: 'Nothing gives me more pleasure than seeing people playing board games through the medium of Welsh, and to know that these games are of the highest quality.'"

"The Great Northern War (1700 - 1721) was a military struggle between Sweden on one side, and Russia, Denmark, and Saxony-Poland on the other."

"In 1708 Charles XII launched an invasion of Russia, determined to capture Moscow and force the Tsar to accept a peace treaty that favored Sweden. Peter the Great ordered a 'scorched earth' policy to deny supplies to the Swedish invaders."

"In September of 1708 Tsar Peter learned that a Swedish force protecting their supply train was separated from Charles XII and the main Swedish army. He pounced. Peter lead a flying column to attack the Swedes while the main part of his army blocked Charles' path to Moscow. The battle took place near the village of Lesnaya."

A miniatures wargame intended for classroom use with two teams of students (grades 6-8). The game is designed to be played on a 7.5x5 foot table using 1/72 scale plastic miniatures. Free print-and-play paper miniatures are also provided for use with the rules.

While the fast play rules are relatively straightforward, they still manage to encompass a number of historical aspects that affected the battle. The rules cover unit formations, terrain, charges, morale, commanders, and differences in the battle tactics of the Russian Dragoons and Swedish cavalry.

Kai Haferkamp's Das Kleine Gespenst won the 2005 Kinderspiel des Jahres, and now the little ghost returns in a smaller, "bring with me" game that maintains the sassy ghost spirit in a new way. The little ghost is hiding somewhere in the box, and the policeman is searching for it, peering into each manhole with a modicum of fear in his heart, waiting to feel the spectre push cold fingers through his forehead and whisk his cerebellum into a frothy pink smoothie.

Ha ha, you believed that? No, the little ghost – should he be under the manhole upon which the policeman stands – will blow off his cap. If you, the player, have been wise enough to be the one who placed the policeman on that manhole, you receive a reward chip. The box is then shaken vigorously in order to spirit the ghost away to some other manhole and await for the policeman's attention there. The first player to blow a cop's hat off five times wins.

Tapp is a regional (Southwestern Germany / Wuerttemberg) traditional trick taking game with 36 Cards (4 suits, french cards from 6 up to Ace, worth 11 (ace), 10 (10), 4 (King), 3 (Queen), 2 (Jack) and zero (6,7,8,9)), with similarities to Skat, Binokel and Tarock. Each player gets eleven cards (3 player-game) three are laid aside as the "talon" or 8 cards (4-player-game) with 4 cards for the "talon". "Coeur" is always highest, the others are equal of value. There are different ways players can decide to "play". The one who "plays" does this against the other players (like in Skat). Usually ("trump game") you need 61 points to win the game.

Description and rules: http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tapp_%28Kartenspiel%29

The player will be able to classify the Officer ranks (O-1 to O-6) of Armed Services including Branch of Service, rank, device worn, and number/arrangement of stripes.

The objective is to get three of a kind, four of a kind, and/or a "straight" (consecutive string of Officer ranks) within any singular specific Armed Service. Each player takes his/her turn in reviewing their own hand, drawing one card from the draw deck, or selecting the first face-up card in the discard deck. Then, he/she discards one unwanted card, and the game proceeds to the next player.
If he has one of demanded combinations he shouts "Salute!" immediately. The remaining players are allowed one more turn to try to match, or exceed the hand of the player who commanded, "SALUTE!".
Every combinations score. The first player to total 500 points wins.

Given a learning objective, the learner will be able to classify it in terms of the content/performance matrix.

The game is similar in several ways to Rummy.

To make a play, the player must hold a minimum of three cards that fit together in the matrix. These may be any combination of "kind," "level," or "objective" cards. For example, three "objective" cards of the same kind or type, two fact "objective" cards and a "FACT" card, or a remember fact "objective" card with a "REMEMBER" card and a "FACT" card. After three cards have been laid down, any related card can be added to the set. To begin another set, a minimum of three cards is required.
When the "draw" pile is used up, the round is over. Each player counts the number of "objective" cards they have played (do not count "kind" or "level" cards) and receives 1 point for each. Players with "objective" cards remaining in their hands must subtract 1 point for each.

Components:

     16 "kind" cards (3 each: Fact, Concept, Procedure, Principle)
     12 "level" cards (3 each: Remember, Use, Find)
     33 "objective" cards

This game is part of the "Lukas Podolski game series

There are green and white circles on the board which are connected with white lines with numbers on it.
Each player gets a deck in one color (black or red) and draws four cards. The player that wins the toss begins the game. To pass the ball the player has to play a card with the number equal or higher than the number on the white lines. If a player plays the ball to a white circle he/she can play another card. Otherwise there is a duel between the two players.
Duel:
Each player has four cards (if a player doesn't have four cards, complete the cards, till you have four) Then both player place a card on the table, face down. When both players have chosen a crad, turn around the cards. The player with the highest number on the card, has ball control. That player can pass the ball along the board.

To score points, the player plays the number (or a higher number) indicated on the board in front of the goal. To keep track of the points, a player receives a goal-chip each time a goal is made. If a player runs out of cards, the player with the most points wins the game.

The object of this game is to try and enter the "Council Fire" while collecting as many 'Honor Beads' as possible while on the way around the board. The player capturing the most points in Honors wins the game. One unusual part of this game is that players all play at the same time. There are no turns in the game, so no waiting for your turn before you play. The winner is the player who captures the most Honors, not neccesarily the person who finishes the game first. You must get to the "Log Cabin Council Fire" by exact count in order to end the game.

This is a card game. There are 3 classes of cards, cards called Sellers, cards with pictures on them called Spectators and money cards. The Seller cards and the Spectator cards are shuffled to form their own decks. The start player draws a Seller card to see what they have to sell and the selling price for the item and then a Spectator card to see if they draw a person willing to buy whats for sale. If they get that combination of an item and a willing seller the player takes the amount of money shown on the Sellers card out of the 'bank'. Some cards show 'no sale' There are also two 'pretty girl' cards, one will buy and one will sell. There is also a 'constable card' which if drawn, the player drawing the card is immediately arrested and so losses his/her turn. When the seller cards are exhausted the game ends and whoever has the most money wins.

Do you have the right cards to be a good cat? To be a bad cat? Can you trick your friends into giving you the cards you need? Do you think you know your friends well enough to know when they’re bluffing? Will you keep them from getting a point or help them?

At the end of the day were you the kind of cat you wanted to be? Everyone will know if you’ve been a good cat or a bad cat!

Good Cat Bad Cat is a game of strategy, bluffing, and paranoia! What else would you expect from a game about cats?

Good Cat Bad Cat is a fast paced game that is easy to learn!

Note: In addition to the cards, you will need 6 CCG sized card sleeves or something similar to use for the card envelopes. A pencil and paper are also needed to keep scores!

Fatal Blow is a skirmish game of miniature combat between individual warriors, fighters and knights.  Fatal Blow is designed to be a fast playing but intriguing game of battle between fantasy and medieval era warriors.  
To most experienced miniature gamers, there is a lot about Fatal Blow that will be familiar, however whether your are an experienced miniature gamer or not you will find a lot about Fatal Blow to set it apart from other skirmish games in this setting.  In Fatal Blow, BOTH the warrior and his weapon(s) will be crucial to his success or failure in any particular combat.  In addition, no matter how powerful a character is, or how weak his opponent may be; if armed with the right weapon, the weaker opponent always has a chance of killing his powerful foe with a single, well laid blow.

Players will be able to correctly match the 50 states in the USA with their capitals.

Object of play is to form matched pairs of states and their capitals. When a player gets rid of all of his or her cards, that player wins the game.

Scoring: The winning player receives 5 points for each card remaining in the other players' hands, whether they form matched pairs or not. Each player, including the winner, receives 10 points for each correctly matched pair and loses 10 points for each incorrectly matched pair that he or she laid down during the game. A list of states and their capitals should be provided to assist players during scoring.

Components:
100 cards
50 states & 50 capitals

The winner of a round is the first person to "go out" as in Rummy, which means that the player can use all the cards in his/her hand to form complete equations. boardgame: The Game of Hickory, Dickory, Dock
description:
Information from gamearchives.org:

Each player selects a piece and begins the game either on the "Start Here" space or the "Or Here" space. Players take turns spinning the indicator and moving up as many places as the indicator shows. Players play in turn trying to reach the UPPER SPACE first. A player must reach the UPPER SPACE by exact count in order to win. "When the clock strikes 1" that is to say, when a player spins a 1, instead of moving forward  one space his piece is immediately dropped to one of the spaces marked "Start Here" and the players piece is obliged to climb again. The only time a 1 does not cause a piece to return to the start space is if the piece is 1 spot away from winning and a 1 would be required to win the game. Then the 1 spin does indeed win the game.

From Gamearchive.org.

The popular story of "Robinson Crusoe" forms the basis for this game for children. In the pack of cards, with which  the game is played are several cards which have exact duplicates, but there is only one card each for Robinson Crusoe, Friday and Rescue Ship. The cards are dealt out one at a time and if the player has two cards that are the same kind, that is 2 Goats, Turtles,  Cannibals etc. the player pairs them up and they are thrown out of the game. Then the start player begins by drawing randomly from the player to his left 1 card. If he pairs a card the pair is removed, Then the next player draws a card. Play continues this way until one player successfully gets the 3 cards CRUSOE,FRIDAY, and RESCUE in his hand whereby winning the game.

In this game there is a board on which are illustrated the castle at Zenda and the palace at Streslau and numerous playing tracks representing the roads between them. There are 3 dice, 4 large pieces and 8 small green pieces who are soldiers. The large red piece represents the king who was the Prizoner of Zenda, 2 large yellow pieces represent Rudolph and Colonel Sapt and the large Green piece represents Black Michael. To win the Zenda player has to capture green pieces with his yellow pieces to open a hole in the green line through which his red piece may slip to Streslau and thus win the game. The Streslau player should try and stop all paths leading to his city by blockade at critical points.

The game centers on a 3-D tree with a plastic trunk and cardboard leaves. Each player gets 3 monkeys in their color (there are 12 monkeys total plus one black chimpanzee figure). Players roll dice to have their monkeys climb the tree via holes in the trunk. If a double is thrown, the player must place the chimpanzee figure a number of holes. Any monkeys the chimpanzee passes must return to the bottom of the tree and start over. The first player to get three monkeys to the top of the tree wins.

A traditional game from Liberia. This game is played by Queah tribe. Game plays on a 5x5 checkered board. Each player has 10 piece in his own color. Game start with an initial start positon with 8 game piece in two different color on board.Pieces moves diagonally as checkers.

On each turn, each player may:
Move one stone to a diagonal adjacent empty cell.
Or, jump (diagonally) over an enemy stone landing on the immediate empty cell. The jumped stone is captured and the other player must place a piece to an empty cell of the board with same color with used cells of board. (if there are in staock). The players only use the squares of the same color).

The aim of this game is capturing all ennemy stones.

This is an attempt to make a game like Seega which is less subject to draws.

This game is played on an empty 5x5 square board. There are 24 double-sided pieces. The game is played in two phases. In the first players alternate placing two pieces at a time with their color showing; any squares may be occupied except the center. In the second phase players alternate moving one piece into the empty square.

A capture is made by sandwiching a single opposing piece between two of one's own. The captured piece is turned over. If at least one capture is made the moving piece may be moved back to the space it came from if that will result in additional captures.

There are two different versions of the game, depending on which rule one uses for the case where a player cannot move because they have no pieces adjacent to the empty space. In version one the player may take any of their pieces and place it in the empty space, but even if they capture they cannot then move it to its original space. In version two the player may turn over a piece adjacent to the empty square; in this case if captures are made the piece may be moved into the empty square if it can make further captures.

In either version the game ends when a player loses by having fewer than five pieces.

The game may also be played by three players using cubes with three colors on them; each player starts with eight of these.

This game is played on a 5x5 board. Game starts with a setup position.
Each player moves one of his stones orthogonally respecting the following restriction:
The moved Black stone must create at least one adjacent area of 3 or 5 empty cells.
The moved White stone must create at least one adjacent area of 2 or 4 empty cells.
The aim of this game is trapping the opponent pieces.
The first player unable to make a move, loses.

The original rules suggest playing on larger boards as well - use 9 pieces per player on a 6x6 board and 14 on a 7x7 board.

This game is played on a 5x5 board. Game starts with an initial setup position. On each turn, each player must move two friendly stones to adjacent (diagonal or orthogonal) empty cells, both in the same direction. Stones cannot move into the restricted points on game board.

The player that first moves 5 stones to the last row wins the game.

This game is played on an empty 1row x n coloumn board with m stones on each side. At each turn, each player must do one of the following actions. Move a stone forward (i.e., left to one player, right to the other) or jump over one enemy stone, landing on the immediate empty cell. The player who makes the last move wins the game.

This game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players start to game with a initial setup position. Each player has 7 game piece in his/her own color.
On each turn, each player must do one of the following things:
Capture a stone on a (orthogonal or diagonal) forward direction by jumping over it and landing on the immediate empty cell. Captures are multiple and mandatory.
Drop a captured friendly stone on any empty cell.
Move a stone on a (orthogonal or diagonal) forward direction to an empty cell.
The player that moves his all game pieces to the opponent's setup position wins the game.

This game was presented at 100 Strategic Games for Pen and Paper by Walter Joris. The game is played on a 4x4 board. Each player has 4 game piece in his/her own color.
At the begining of the game each player alternately, place eight friendly stones each.
During just four turns, each player shifts one row left/right, one column up/down or one of the two main diagonals, just by one cell. 
Every stone pushed out the board is removed from play.
After those four turns (i.e., eight shifts), The player with more stones on board wins the game (or a draw if equal).

This game is played on a 3x4 rectangular board. Each player has 5 pieces in his/her own color.
At the begining of the game each player alternately place a stone into an empty cell exept one restricted cell.
After all cells occupied, On each turn, each player must do one of the following actions:
Capture an adjacent stone on an orthogonal direction by jumping over it and landing on the immediate empty cell. Captures are multiple and mandatory or move a piece on an orthogonal direction to an empty cell.
The player that captures all enemy stones, or has his last stone on the restricted cell wins the game.

This is a traditional 2-player game from West Africa, played on a 5x6 rectangular board. Each player has 12 game pieces in her own color, which start off the board; traditionally one player uses small sticks, the other uses pebbles.
Players initially take turns to place ('drop') one of their pieces on the board.  Each must be dropped on an empty space on the board.  One source states that this must not be adjacent (orthogonally) to a friendly piece, but the original descriptions by Monot [1950], translated by Murray [1951] only state that - during this stage - no player may form a row of three of her own pieces.  If a player cannot make a legal drop, she must pass. This continues until all pieces are placed on the board, or until no more pieces can be placed.
After that, in each turn and starting with the player who began the game, each player moves one of their own pieces to an empty adjacent (orthogonal) space on the board. If, by so doing, she is able to make an orthogonal  row of exactly three of her own pieces, she may capture one enemy piece (of her choice, presumably).  The player who captures all of her opponent's pieces wins the game, and scores two points if this is achieved without any of her own being captured, or one point if she has lost at least one of her own pieces.  A series of matches is played, and the series is won by the first player to score 10 points.

Chicken Roundabout is a board game inspired by the 'Chicken Roundabout' in in Ditchingham, near Bungay (Suffolk, UK) a roundabout (traffic circle) famous for the chickens which can always be seen there.

The game is basically an enhanced version of Pachisi/Ludo, with the addition of 'tokens' required to get the chickens home. In this version, opponents' chickens can not be sent back to the start by overtaking them.

The game was previously marketed as 'Us Chickens'. The new version differs by having local businesses featured on the spaces on the board.

From the publisher:
Dance along to Hannah Montana's awesome songs! This cool Twister Moves game allows you to listen to her music and move as the DJ tells you to! Put the CD in your machine, set out the mats and away you go! Listen carefully to the instructions and follow the fun moves! If you're the only one to get the moves right, you win! Practice on your own and then compete against friends! A wonderful way to encourage active play! Great for any Hannah Montana fan!

From the publisher:
Manufacturer's Description
BrainBox Manchester United is a game for the whole family. The game is ingeniously simple. A player has ten seconds to study a card that shows players past and present in action and is then asked a question about the card, chosen by the roll of a die. If the card is answered correctly the card is kept, if not it is returned to the box. The most cards held after ten minutes decides the winner. Cards are real photos of players, with extra information like how many caps the player has, the year of their International debut and what shirt number they wear. Hard core fans can put their knowledge to the test--especially if their powers of observation let them down. The game is presented in a smart cube box and is a perfect gift for Man U fans who already have everything.
family: Sports Teams: Manchester United Football Club

description:
From the publisher:
 The object of the game is to win as many cards as possible in ten minutes. The first player takes a card from the BrainBox and turns the timer over. The player then has ten seconds to study the picture on the card. When the timer runs out the card is passed to player two and player one rolls the die to see which question is to be asked. The questions are on the reverse of the picture card. Player two checks the answer by looking at the picture and if it is correct the card is given back to player one to keep. If the answer is wrong the card is placed back into the BrainBox and the next player chooses a card. Please remember, the answer to the question is on the picture side of the card, so look carefully! The player with the most cards after ten minutes wins! If there is a tie, it's sudden death with both players choosing a card and having to answer all eight questions on the card. The player who answers the most correctly wins! For 1 or more players, ages 8+. Contains 54 illustrated cards, 1 rules card, 1 timer and 1 eight sided die. Have fun!

Box Contains

     1 rules card
     1 sand timer
     1 eight-sided die

What can you spot in ten seconds? Turn the timer over and try and memorize as many pictures and words as you can. After ten seconds, pass the card to another player who asks a quick-fire question. Developed in conjunction with London’s National Gallery, BrainBox Art is full of interesting facts and amazing images of some of the best paintings in the world in stunning high quality on 56 beautifully-illustrated cards.

Includes all 44 Presidents from Washigton to Lincoln, JFK to Obama. Can we do this...Yes we can!

Loups Gris en Atlantique (Grey Wolves in Atlantic) is a single player board-wargame which simulates German patrols of a U-Boot during World War II.

The player is a U-Boot commander and his mission is to sink a maximum of allies ships during the beginning of 1941.
He has choice between 4 types of submarines (VII, IX.B, IX.C, IX.D2) with their own specificities and he can personnalize his character and crew with points.

Rules are easy and, like many solitaire wargames, needs a lot of dice rolls.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Wyx is a 2 to 4 player game where each player control an ant queen on a 8 by 8 squares board.
After each move the queen lay an ant egg, althoug on some square she may lay more than one.
The moves are done by chosing from 4 columns of dominos, the top of each column being visble. Each dominos have a vertical and horizontal values indicating the move the queen will have to execute.

The aim of the game is to be the first player to lay out its 16 eggs.

The Easter Bunny Game is a pickup and delivery game in which players of all ages move their bunnies around the board, collecting Easter eggs, and delivering them to homes in time for Easter.

The game is played with chocolate mini-eggs; approximately 24 in four colours are required each game.

If I get the balance right, older kids and adults will enjoy the challenge of planning efficient routes and beating the opponents to the more lucrative destinations, while younger kids will still enjoy scoffing the eggs :)

Ingredients:
12 cards
Board
4 counters
24 miniature chocolate eggs... you'll need a new supply for each game!

Notice: I have removed the old rules and files due to there being a completely new version in the works, and the previous versions had too many "bugs".

A game full of less brains and more shooting. Create your character, grab some items, and destroy your buddies. Build your play area however you want, using as many or as few obstacles and pickups as desired!

Gameplay consists of drawing weapon and equipment cards as well as special cards to help break the rules. From Death Matches to trying to be the Last Man Standing, there are several match types to suit different styles of play.

Move around the play area by rolling dice and measuring out the distance. It's like a single man skirmish with you trying to be the winning player as you complete the terms of victory for various match types.

Get ready for the battle of the ages when you go head to head in a series of dinosaur duels!

Is your Stegosaurus strong enough to defeat your opponent's Apatosaurus?

Let the fun and the dino duels begin!

The Dino Duel Card Game is one dino-mite game to play! Players lay down their cards in a duel. On the top of the cards is located the point value. If your Stegosaurus card contains more points than your opponent's T-Rex card, then you win that set of cards. The cards feature the Tyrannosaurus Rex, Apatosaurus, Stegosaurus, and wild cards. Any number of combinations can create different outcomes. The player with the most sets wins!

Contains 48 cards (measuring 4 1/2" x 3") and instructions. No reading required to play.

Flick Pool is a dexterity game distributed in Whataburger kids meals. It consists of round tiles and triangular "pockets". Players take turns flicking the Cue Ball into other balls to try to get them in the pockets. It's just like pool, but smaller and flatter.

As players roll the die, they make their way around a colorful racetrack. When they land on different colored spaces, players choose cards that ask them to figure out parts of speech, noun plurals, verb tenses, blends, homophones, definitions, idioms, context clues, anagrams and more. As players correctly answer questions, they power ahead on the track. Teachers can choose how many laps to complete to adapt to time constraints. Covers the following aspects of Bloom's Taxonomy: Knowledge, Comprehension, Application, Analysis

*  Compact size saves space on the shelf and in the classroom
    *  High-interest graphics and content
    *  Short passages allow time for full comprehension and the chance to read a variety of topics
    *  Winning is based on knowledge and skills, not random factors
    *  Games address important content, not trivia
    *  Directions are easy to understand, so as not to restrict learning opportunities
    *  Players never lose points for wrong answers

Gauntlet - From the Klix Pocket Travel Games from Tiger Electronics.

The board folds out, the magnetic pieces (which function as both player pieces and tokens for the potion track) are stored in a built-in compartment and the dice are self-contained in a see-through compartment. The dice are 'rolled' by pushing and releasing the 'dice snapper'.

The board itself is broken into four sections: "Room 1", "Room 2", "Room 1-3 Treasure Room" and "Room 4". The players take turns moving through the rooms in order, landing on one of four kinds of space: a blank space, a space with food on it (which adds to the players food/HP), a space with a ghost on it (which subtracts from the same), and a space with a bomb (which advances the players 'potion track' by one). The final space on the Room 4 board contains the 'Sacred Orb'.
family: Series: Klix Pocket Travel Games (Tiger Electronics)

description:
One of the "Easyplay Kids" series. This one is a simple dice-roller.

Each player has a set of five 6-sided dice showing one of five different monsters and a rocket. One monster is revealed at random from a deck of five cards and players simultaneously roll their dice (one at a time). The round ends when one player has four dice matching the monster on the card and the fifth die does not show the rocket.

The losers pay the winner in "Mars Maggot" tokens (to feed the monster). If they can't afford to pay then they must discard a "Rocket" token. The first player to lose all their rocket tokens loses the game; the Player with the most "Mars Maggot´s" win.

boardgame: Battle with the Swedes for Charles Bridge
description:
From the rules:  "In the course of the cruel Thirty Years' War, the people inhabiting the Czech Lands were subjected to many invasions by Swedish mercenaries who looted burghers' houses, aristocratic palaces, churches, libraries and monasteries.  Regardless of whether they were led by Generals Baner, Torstenson or Wrangel, the Swedes always wanted to take away as much as they could . . . On the night of July 25, 1648, the Swedish cavalry tried to conquer the Stone Bridge (today's Charles Bridge) and penetrate the Old Town through the gate in the Old Town bridge tower . . .The people of Prague, and particularly students led by Father Jiri Plachy, quickly took up arms and rushed to help the tiny unit of the tower guards.  A fierce hand-to-hand fight ensued, with the fighters on both sides hiding behind the the statues on the bridge.  The battle took the toll of many dead and wounded on both sides.  The present game is based on this particular battle with all its snares and personal fights."

The rules are in Czech, English, German and French.  The board is a simple checkerboard-type surface on medium-weight cardboard.  The only other components are 28 small wooden blocks, 14 dyed red, 14 in their natural light brown coloring. There is a coin on the front of the box that is tossed at the start of the game to determine how many moves the Swedish player gets.

Each player places their 14 men on the first two rows of squares on each side of the board. The men can move one square in 12 possible directions.  You eliminate your opponent's men by landing on their square and can take another turn provided you can eliminate another man with that move.  All men can jump over each other.  The player who has more of his men on the starting line of his rival when no one can make any more moves wins.

A special expansion tile available at LeiriaCon 2010 exclusive of Spiel Portugal and later re-released at Spiel 2013 in Essen

The first player to fill his 6 spaces enclosure gets the lynx tile and puts it next to his barn. The players keep the Lynx until the end of the game.

At the end of the game, after the last truck is chosen and before the scoring phase, the owner of the Lynx may carry out one extra money action. He still has to pay the cost for this action.

Expands:

    Zooloretto


Home Page & Newsletter:

     www.zooloretto.com 


(German, english, dutch, hungarian, spanish, swedish)

This is a strategic placement game wherein each of 2 to 4 players begins with 25 regular 6-sided dice. In the center of the playing surface is a cross of dice ("the wall" - see diagram) against which dice are placed to score. Each die placed must touch two other dice, and only dice touching the same number in the wall in an uninterrupted perpendicular alignment will score. (Two players may begin with 36 dice each if available.)

Every player gets 20 cards in his/her color. Try to get a product together with four cards.
The objective of the game is to put the products on the board as favorable as possible. The player who puts his product on the middle of the board gets the most points.
The player throw two dice and put his/her two pawns on the grid of the board. Where the two pawns come together, a player can put a card of his/her product.
If there is already a card, a player can change the card (only if he/she is the owner of the product). Or can try to get the space for his own product.(players throw dice to get the space)
When a player has placed all his/her products on the board, the counting begins. The player with the most points wins the game.

There are 80 cards with questions at the front of the card. At the back of the cards there are collered lines (the ski arenas). The group or player who gets the longest line, wins the game.

The question are truth or style. If a player answers a question correctly or follows the instructions , he/she gets the card. Otherwise the card is put back in the box.

Contents:
- 80 cards
- 1 CD (just for fun)

boardgame: Le Franc Tireur #12: PTO and Chinese Civil War for ASL
description:
80 color pages, articles mainly analyze beach landings in both the ETO and PTO. Includes map LFT 2 (very large hill), two ASL solo scenarios, and 10 ASL scenarios, focusing on the Pacific Theater and the Chinese Civil War, almost all in English.

Scenarios:

     SASL FT-1 Hit Them Hard: You play Nationalist Chinese forces trained by the Germans involved in a counter-attack in Shanghai, 15 August 1937.
     SASL FT-2 Against Ill Advice: You play Japanese army troops sent ahead in Malaya to defend a beach against British counterattacks.
     ASL FT 136 Shanghai by Sea: Nationalist Chinese versus Japanese Shanghai Expeditionary Force making a beach assault in Wusong (south of Shanghai), 23 August 1937.
     ASL FT 137 300:  Last stand in Shanghai of the Nationalist Chinese 88th Division (German-trained) against the Japanese Shanghai Expeditionary Force, 30 October 1937.
     ASL FT 138 Meeting Up at Matan:  Nationalist Chinese against Japan's Formosa Infantry Brigade west of Wuhan, 24 June 1938.
     ASL FT 139 Ride of the 200th:  Japanese Infantry versus Nationalist Chinese armor in Kunlun Pass in south China, 19 December 1939.
     ASL FT 140 Chinese Raiders:  Nationalist Chinese irregular forces fighting in Vietnam against Vichy French helped by Meos partisan forces, 1-2 June 1942.
     ASL FT 141 Easy Day at Volupai:  Japanese infantry versus U.S. marines in New Britain, 6 March 1944.
     ASL FT 142 Cut, Slash and Mow Down: U.S. infantry defend against elements of the crack Japanese 6th infantry on Bougainville, 24 March 1944.
     ASL FT 143 Red Scare:  Fighting between Nationalist and Communist Chinese forces in North China, 13 November 1948.
     ASL FT 144 Tigers of Pantang: Fighting between Nationalist and Communist Chinese in North China, 16 November 1948.
     ASL FT 145 Bears of Kinmen: Nationalist infantry defend against Communist Chinese beach assaults on Kinmen Island, just off the coast of Fukien province, 25 October 1949.
